title: Add follow-up cadence setting and the due-postings store query
userStory: As the user, I want scout to compute which postings are overdue for a follow-up based on a configurable day interval, so the right postings surface automatically without manual upkeep.
acceptanceCriteria: A DB-backed singleton setting `follow_up_interval_days` exists (integer, default 7), following the existing singleton pattern (e.g. taste_filter / email-template), with store get/set accessors.

A store query ListFollowUpsDue() returns postings where outreach_status = 'awaiting' AND last_outreach_at is non-null AND last_outreach_at <= (today - interval days) AND response = '' AND the posting is not hidden/rejected.
Results are sorted most-overdue-first (oldest last_outreach_at first) and include enough fields to render the queue: company name, title, last_outreach_at, days overdue, contacts, and posting id/url.
Postings with outreach_status 'replied' or 'no_response' are excluded; postings with a non-empty response are excluded.
Changing follow_up_interval_days changes which postings the query returns (a posting due at interval=7 is not due at interval=14, given the same last_outreach_at).
Boundary check: a posting whose last_outreach_at is exactly `today - interval` is included (>= the threshold); `today - interval + 1` is not.
A store-level test covers the inclusion/exclusion rules, the interval sensitivity, and the ordering.
